endpoints.name: Create an account holder
endpoints.description: Creates an account holder linked to a [legal entity](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/legalentity/latest/post/legalEntities).

createAccountHolder.description: Example request for creating an account holder

value.description: Liable account holder used for international payments and payouts

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get an account holder
endpoints.description: Returns an account holder.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update an account holder
endpoints.description: Updates an account holder. When updating an account holder resource, if a parameter is not provided in the request, it is left unchanged.

requestAccountHolderCapability.description: Example request for the `receivePayments` capability for an account holder

value.description: Liable account holder used for international payments and payouts

updateAccountHolderStatus.description: Example request for permanently deactivating an account holder

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all balance accounts of an account holder
endpoints.description:
Returns a paginated list of the balance accounts associated with an account holder. To fetch multiple pages, use the query parameters. 

For example, to limit the page to 5 balance accounts and skip the first 10, use `/accountHolders/{id}/balanceAccounts?limit=5&offset=10`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Create a balance account
endpoints.description: Creates a balance account that holds the funds of the associated account holder.

createBalanceAccount.description: Example request for creating a balance account

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a balance account
endpoints.description: Returns a balance account and its balances for the default currency and other currencies with a non-zero balance.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a balance account
endpoints.description: Updates a balance account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all payment instruments for a balance account
endpoints.description:
Returns a paginated list of the payment instruments associated with a balance account. 

To fetch multiple pages, use the query parameters.For example, to limit the page to 3 payment instruments and to skip the first 6, use `/balanceAccounts/{id}/paymentInstruments?limit=3&offset=6`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get a balance platform
endpoints.description: Returns a balance platform.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all account holders under a balance platform
endpoints.description:
Returns a paginated list of all the account holders that belong to the balance platform. To fetch multiple pages, use the query parameters. 

For example, to limit the page to 5 account holders and to skip the first 20, use `/balancePlatforms/{id}/accountHolders?limit=5&offset=20`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get a document
endpoints.description: Returns a document.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a document
endpoints.description: Deletes a document.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a document
endpoints.description: Updates a document.

updateDocument.description: Example request for updating a document

value.description: Proof of industry doc for Example Company

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Create a legal entity
endpoints.description:
Creates a legal entity. 

This resource contains information about the user that will be onboarded in your platform. Adyen uses this information to perform verification checks as required by payment industry regulations. Adyen informs you of the verification results through webhooks or API responses.

createLegalEntityIndividualNL.description: Example request for creating a legal entity for an individual residing in the Netherlands
createLegalEntityIndividualNL.summary: Create a legal entity for an individual residing in the Netherlands

createLegalEntityIndividualUS.description: Example request for creating a legal entity for an individual residing in the US
createLegalEntityIndividualUS.summary: Create a legal entity for an individual residing in the US

createLegalEntityOrganizationNL.description: Example request for creating a legal entity for an organization operating in the Netherlands
createLegalEntityOrganizationNL.summary: Create a legal entity for a Dutch organization

createLegalEntityOrganizationUS.description: Example request for creating a legal entity for an organization operating in the US
createLegalEntityOrganizationUS.summary: Create a legal entity for an organization in the US

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a legal entity
endpoints.description: Returns a legal entity.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a legal entity
endpoints.description:
Updates a legal entity.

 >To change the legal entity type, include only the new `type` in your request. To update the `entityAssociations` array, you need to replace the entire array. For example, if the array has 3 entries and you want to remove 1 entry, you need to PATCH the resource with the remaining 2 entries.

changeLegalEntityType.description: Example request when changing the legal entity type
changeLegalEntityType.summary: Change a legal entity from an organization to an individual

updateLegalEntityOrganization.description: Example request adding entity associations to a legal entity
updateLegalEntityOrganization.summary: Add entity associations to an organization

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Create a payment instrument group
endpoints.description: Creates a payment instrument group to associate and group payment instrument resources together. You can apply a transaction rule to a payment instrument group.

createPaymentInstrumentGroups.description: Example request for creating a payment instrument group

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a payment instrument group
endpoints.description: Returns the details of a payment instrument group.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all transaction rules for a payment instrument group
endpoints.description: Returns a list of all the transaction rules associated with a payment instrument group.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Create a payment instrument
endpoints.description:
Creates a payment instrument to issue a physical card, a virtual card, or a business account to your user.

 For more information, refer to [Issue cards](https://docs.adyen.com/issuing/create-cards) or [Issue business accounts](https://docs.adyen.com/marketplaces-and-platforms/business-accounts).

createBusinessAccountNL.description: Example request for creating a business account in NL
createBusinessAccountNL.summary: Create a business account in NL

createBusinessAccountUS.description: Example request for creating a business account in the US
createBusinessAccountUS.summary: Create a business account in the US

createPhysicalCard.description: Example request for creating a physical card

createVirtualCard.description: Example request for creating a virtual card

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a payment instrument
endpoints.description: Returns the details of a payment instrument.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a payment instrument
endpoints.description: Updates a payment instrument. Once a payment instrument is already active, you can only update its status. However, for cards created with **inactive** status, you can still update the balance account associated with the card.

updatePaymentInstrumentBalanceAccount.description: Example request for updating the balance account of a payment instrument
updatePaymentInstrumentBalanceAccount.summary: Update the balance account linked to a payment instrument

updatePaymentInstrumentStatus.description: Example request for updating the status of a payment instrument
updatePaymentInstrumentStatus.summary: Update the status of a payment instrument

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get the PAN of a payment instrument
endpoints.description:
Returns the primary account number (PAN) of a payment instrument.

To make this request, your API credential must have the following [role](https://docs.adyen.com/issuing/manage-access/api-credentials-web-service#api-permissions):

* Balance Platform BCL PCI role
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Get all transaction rules for a payment instrument
endpoints.description: Returns a list of transaction rules associated with a payment instrument.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Create a transaction rule
endpoints.description: Creates a [transaction rule](https://docs.adyen.com/issuing/transaction-rules). When your user makes a transaction with their Adyen-issued card, the transaction is allowed or declined based on the conditions and outcome defined in the transaction rule. You can apply the transaction rule to several cards, such as all the cards in your platform, or to a specific card. For use cases, see [examples](https://docs.adyen.com/issuing/transaction-rules/examples).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a transaction rule
endpoints.description: Returns the details of a transaction rule.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{TRANSACTIONRULEID}} with a real transactionruleid.

endpoints.name: Delete a transaction rule
endpoints.description: Deletes a transaction rule.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{TRANSACTIONRULEID}} with a real transactionruleid.

endpoints.name: Update a transaction rule
endpoints.description:
Updates a transaction rule. 

* To update only the status of a transaction rule, send only the `status` parameter. All other parameters not provided in the request are left unchanged.

* When updating any other parameter, you need to send all existing resource parameters. If you omit a parameter in the request, that parameter is removed from the resource.

updateTransactionRuleStatus.description: Example request for updating the status of a transaction rule
updateTransactionRuleStatus.summary: Update the status of transaction rule

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{TRANSACTIONRULEID}} with a real transactionruleid.

endpoints.name: Create a transfer instrument
endpoints.description:
Creates a transfer instrument. 

A transfer instrument is a bank account that a legal entity owns. Adyen performs verification checks on the transfer instrument as required by payment industry regulations. We inform you of the verification results through webhooks or API responses.

When the transfer instrument passes the verification checks, you can start sending funds from the balance platform to the transfer instrument (such as payouts).

createTransferInstrumentEUR.description: Example request for creating a transfer instrument for EUR

createTransferInstrumentUSD.description: Example request for creating a transfer instrument for USD

endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a transfer instrument
endpoints.description: Returns the details of a transfer instrument.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Delete a transfer instrument
endpoints.description: Deletes a transfer instrument.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.

endpoints.name: Update a transfer instrument
endpoints.description: Updates a transfer instrument.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADYEN_API_KEY}} with your ADYEN API KEY. Replace {{ID}} with a real id.
